raw_12_pre_registration.criterion: |Δadvantage_per_thousand[k] - advantage_per_thousand[k-1]| < 5 across two consecutive ticks → CONVERGED at the smaller N_TRAIN

hypotheses.H1_coupling: advantage_per_thousand > 50 at ALL N_TRAIN ticks (15, 50, 150, 500, 1500) — state space exceeds bigram capacity, Law 64 structural on 40x40
hypotheses.H2_uncoupled: advantage_per_thousand converges to 0 just like 10x10, by N_TRAIN ≤ 1500 — Markov universal
hypotheses.AMBIGUOUS: advantage drops but plateaus above 0 and below 50, OR convergence criterion never met

setup.test_set_window: trailing 15 pairs after step max_train (identical for all ticks; same ladder as T8i)
setup.execution_environment: Mac local CPU (raw 42) — wallclock ~1 min, well within axis-sweep budget

verdict.label: AMBIGUOUS_partial_coupling
verdict.convergence_failure_reason: Δ=17 between n=500→1500 broke stability streak (a near-converge at n=150-500 was followed by another large drop)
verdict.interpretation: The +22% asymptote on 40x40/N_TRAIN=15 (commit 95b43ddb) is mostly a TRAIN-VOLUME ARTIFACT, but NOT entirely. Markov drops 935 → 996 (out of 1000) but does not fully reach parity within N_TRAIN=1500. Trajectory is still falling at the rightmost tick — orbit period exceeds 1500 but is approaching capture. Coupling between train-volume and grid axes is PARTIAL: larger grids buy you slower Markov saturation but not eternal advantage.

law_64_v4_update.v3_was: Law 64: advantage of CA(5) over Markov order-1 grows with grid size (CONVERGED at 40x40 ≈ 80x80 / +22%) and peaks at 32% density
law_64_v4_update.v4_revised: Law 64 v4 (post-T8j coupling falsification): The CA(5)-vs-Markov-order-1 advantage on Conway is INTRINSICALLY a TRAIN-VOLUME-DEPENDENT phenomenon at all tested grid sizes. Within 1500 training pairs, advantage decays from 69→4 per_thousand on 40x40/32% (parallel pattern to 10x10 but slower). The advantage axis cannot be cleanly separated from the train-volume axis; H1 (structural eternal advantage) is FALSIFIED for 40x40/32%/seed=20260428. Forward open question: do (i) larger grids (80x80, 160x160), (ii) aperiodic initial patterns, or (iii) higher-density/chaotic regimes restore eternal advantage, or does Markov order-1 always saturate given enough training? The v3 framing 'advantage CONVERGES at +22% on 40x40' must be qualified as 'ASYMPTOTE WITHIN A TRAIN-VOLUME WINDOW' — at fixed N_TRAIN=15. Train-volume IS a coupled axis everywhere we have tested.

axes_now_understood.grid: amplifies advantage at fixed low N_TRAIN; effect attenuates as N_TRAIN→1500
axes_now_understood.density: peaks at 32% across all tested grids
axes_now_understood.train_volume: MONOTONICALLY ERODES advantage on every grid tested; convergence speed grid-size-dependent (10x10 fully converges by n=150; 40x40 still falling at n=1500)
axes_now_understood.pattern: still unswept — forward step (aperiodic initial conditions may sustain advantage)

raw_91_caveats.C1_execution: Mac local CPU per raw 42 — single-pass, ~60s wallclock, no GPU/witness ledger required
raw_91_caveats.C2_test_set: Held-out test fixed at trailing 15 pairs of 1515-step trajectory — identical across all 5 N_TRAIN ticks AND directly comparable to T8i (10x10) ladder
raw_91_caveats.C3_orbit_periodicity: Conway is deterministic; 40x40/32% likely entering periodic orbit at higher N_TRAIN (Markov tf_acc rising from 935→996 indicates redundant pair coverage). Period appears longer than 1500 steps but capture is in progress. This is the structural reason H1 fails: Markov DOES eventually catch up; only timescale differs by grid size.
raw_91_caveats.C4_capacity_scaling: Order-1 per-cell bigram has 4 counts × 1600 cells = 6400 capacity on 40x40 vs 400 on 10x10. Advantage timescale scales sub-linearly with capacity (10x10 saturates by n=150; 40x40 still descending at n=1500 — ~10x slower). This rate suggests larger grids may extend window without ever providing eternal advantage.
raw_91_caveats.C5_single_seed: Single seed (20260428). AMBIGUOUS verdict at one seed is honest but weak; multi-seed (≥5) sweep at 40x40/32%/n=1500 is forward step T8k. If multi-seed bands span all three verdicts, AMBIGUOUS hardens; if all converge below 50/1000, H2 firms up.
raw_91_caveats.C6_forward_extension: Predicted forward steps: (T8k) multi-seed at 40x40/32%/n=1500 to harden verdict; (T8l) extend 80x80 to N_TRAIN=1500 — does even larger grid restore eternal advantage?; (T8m) aperiodic init pattern test (gliders only, no symmetric noise) — periodicity may be the dominant cause

raws_compliance.raw_12_frozen_pre_registration: PASS — H1/H2/AMBIGUOUS declared in tool header before --run; verdict mapping pre-coded
raws_compliance.raw_91_honesty: PASS — verdict reports AMBIGUOUS honestly, refuses to spin partial coupling as either H1 or H2; explicit caveats on single-seed, periodic orbit, capacity scaling
